reasoning: The Polymarket price for this market on October 30, 2025, was 0.35. A thorough search of news up to November 9, 2025, revealed no qualifying military encounters having occurred since the market opened on October 8. While NATO forces did engage Russian Shahed drones that violated Polish airspace in September and early October, the resolution criteria clearly state that intercepting loitering munitions targeting a 3rd party does not qualify. No manned aircraft or non-munition UAVs have been shot down, nor have there been direct missile strikes or artillery exchanges between NATO and Russian forces. There were no new major escalatory incidents between October 30 and November 9. Thus, the risk profile is stable, but approximately 10 days closer to the resolution date. I apply a small time-decay adjustment to the 0.35 market price, leading to a forecast of 0.33. This perfectly reflects the sustained, elevated tensions (which justify a relatively high ~30% probability) while accounting for the lack of a triggering event to date.
